launch.description: Extension: how to foreground-launch a server not spawned by the client itself (e.g. a remote http/sse server). Not executed automatically

command.description: Executable to run in the foreground to start the server

cwd.description: Working directory for the launched process

env.description: Environment variables passed to the launched process

server.description: The de facto 'mcpServers' entry shape shared by Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and Zed. No official schema is published for this shape, so this is best-effort.

command.description: Executable to launch for a stdio server

cwd.description: Working directory for the stdio process, part of the MCP stdio transport spec. Not reliably honored by real-world clients — verify before relying on it.

env.description: Environment variables passed to the stdio server process. Claude Code expands ${VAR} / ${VAR:-default} here; Claude Desktop does not

headers.description: HTTP headers sent to a remote (http/sse) server, e.g. Authorization

type.description: Transport type. Omit for stdio servers (implied by 'command')

description: MCP client config file: a 'mcpServers' map, plus repo-local 'disabled'/'setup'/'launch' extensions.

disabled.description: Extension: when true, no server in this file is applied.

setup.description: Extension: manual prep steps. Informational only, not executed automatically

title: MCP client config (mcpServers)
